title: Achyronia marginalis
intro: Achyronia marginalis, also known as the Marginal Speedwell, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Campanulaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular and immune system health. Bioactive constituents of Achyronia marginalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Achyronia marginalis is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Achyronia marginalis is a member of the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Achyronia, which is a monotypic genus consisting of this single species.
